message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Bizarre Lineage Boxing: Best Build & Fighting Style Guide 2026",
  description: "Master Bizarre Lineage boxing with our comprehensive guide. Learn trainer locations, quest steps, movesets, and the best Stand pairings for 2026.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Bizarre+Lineage+Boxing+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Bizarre Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Primary Style**: Bizarre lineage boxing is the premier choice for close-range combo enthusiasts and PvP beginners.
- **Trainer Location**: Head to the second floor of the Gym located near **Bus Stop 2** in Morioh Cho.
- **Key Mechanic**: The Dempsey Roll serves as a powerful counter move that punishes aggressive opponents.
- **Best Pairings**: Boxing works exceptionally well with high-damage Stands like **Star Platinum** and **Crazy Diamond**.
- **Requirement**: You must have **$100** in cash to initiate the questline with the Boxing Coach.
</Callout>

In the competitive landscape of Morioh Cho, choosing the right fighting style is the difference between victory and a quick trip back to the spawn point. Among the available options, **bizarre lineage boxing** stands out as the most reliable and beginner-friendly combat discipline for players who prefer a brawling playstyle. This style focuses on high-speed stuns, relentless M1 chain extensions, and reactive counters that can turn the tide of any duel. 

Whether you are a new player looking for an accessible entry into PvP or a veteran seeking to optimize a heavy-hitting Stand build, mastering **bizarre lineage boxing** provides the mechanical foundation necessary for high-level play in 2026. In this guide, we will break down the exact steps to unlock the style, analyze the complete moveset, and provide the most effective Stand synergies to dominate the current meta.

<Section icon={Map} title="Trainer Location and Initial Requirements">

Finding the Boxing Coach is the first step in your journey toward pugilistic mastery. Unlike some trainers hidden in obscure corners of the map, the boxing trainer is located in a central, high-traffic area, making it the easiest style to locate early in your progression.

**Video Highlights:**
- Visual guide to finding the Gym at Bus Stop 2.
- Walkthrough of the second-floor layout to find the Boxing Coach.
- Brief showcase of the boxing M1 and M2 animation changes.
- Overview of the $100 entry fee requirement.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="0rkwFx8FjLw" title="Bizarre Lineage Boxing Location & Showcase" />

The trainer is situated on the second floor of the Gym, which is conveniently located right next to **Bus Stop 2**. Before you speak to him, ensure you have at least $100 in your inventory. This fee is mandatory to start the questline and cannot be bypassed.

| Requirement | Detail | Importance |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Cash** | $100 | Mandatory to start the quest |
| **Location** | Bus Stop 2 (Gym) | Primary trainer hub |
| **NPC** | Boxing Coach | Found on the 2nd floor |
| **Level** | Recommended 15+ | Ensures you can handle the quest NPCs |

<Callout type="warning" title="Financial Tip">
Do not spend your early-game cash on cosmetics or unnecessary items until you have secured your fighting style. The $100 fee is a flat rate, but failing to complete the quest may require additional preparation.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Mastering Bizarre Lineage Boxing: Moveset & Mechanics">

The core appeal of **bizarre lineage boxing** lies in its ability to stun enemies and create openings for your Stand's heavy attacks. Equipping this style alters your basic M1 and M2 animations, giving you faster, more fluid punches that integrate seamlessly into Stand combos.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="White Fang [E]">
    - **Multi-hit combo**
    - High stun potential
    - Ideal combo starter
  </Card>
  <Card title="Liver Crusher [R]">
    - **Quick double punch**
    - Interrupts enemy moves
    - Fast recovery time
  </Card>
  <Card title="Dempsey Roll [F]">
    - **Reactive counter**
    - Massive burst damage
    - Rewards patient play
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

The moveset is designed for pressure. **White Fang** delivers a series of hooks and an uppercut that leaves the opponent momentarily incapacitated. **Liver Crusher** is your primary tool for punishing "whiffed" attacks from your opponent, providing a quick stun at a very short range. Finally, the **Dempsey Roll** is the signature move of the style; it allows you to sway and automatically counter-attack if hit, dealing significant damage.

| Move Name | Keybind | Damage Tier | Utility |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **White Fang** | [E] | High | Stun/Opener |
| **Liver Crusher** | [R] | Medium | Interrupt/Punish |
| **Dempsey Roll** | [F] | Very High | Counter/Finisher |
| **Haymaker** | [Z] | Medium | Guard Break |

<Callout type="tip" title="Combo Secret">
The best way to use Boxing is to lead with an M1 string, cancel into **White Fang**, and then immediately summon your Stand for a barrage. This maximizes the stun window provided by the boxing moves.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Step-by-Step Unlock Guide">

Unlocking the boxing fighting style requires more than just money; you must prove your worth through a series of combat trials. The questline is designed to test your mastery of basic combat mechanics and your ability to handle both PvE and PvP scenarios.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Initiate the Quest">
    Visit the Boxing Coach at the Bus Stop 2 Gym. Pay the $100 fee to begin the trial. He will task you with defeating a specific number of local NPCs to test your stamina.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="The Sparring Session">
    After the NPC kills, you must spar with the Boxing Master himself. This is a controlled fight where you need to demonstrate your ability to block and land hits consistently.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Raid Boss Challenge">
    You will be required to participate in a Raid Boss event. For the fastest completion, head to **Bus Stop 14** and defeat **Avid**. This boss is relatively easy and usually takes less than a minute with a decent Stand.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="PvP & World Events">
    Winning 1v1 duels is a core requirement. You can use the PvP mission board or ask a friend to help you clear this step. Additionally, you must complete at least one World Event that appears randomly across the map.
  </Step>
  <Step num="5" title="Defeat Your Inner Self">
    The final trial involves meditating at the Gym to fight your own "Bonkey" (a clone of yourself). This mirror match tests your knowledge of your own build's weaknesses.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Callout type="success" title="Efficiency Note">
When hunting for the Raid Boss, check the server list for active boss indicators. Avid at Bus Stop 14 is the community favorite for quest completion due to its low health pool and predictable attack patterns.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Best Stand Pairings for Boxing">

While boxing is powerful on its own, it truly shines when paired with a Stand that can capitalize on short-range stuns. Because boxing has a limited range (rated 3/10), you need a Stand that thrives in the "pocket" or has gap-closing abilities.

| Stand | Synergy Rating | Why it Works |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Star Platinum** | ★★★★★ | Boxing stuns guarantee successful Star Finger and Ora Barrages. |
| **Crazy Diamond** | ★★★★★ | The "Rock Trap" combined with Liver Crusher creates an infinite stun loop. |
| **The World** | ★★★★☆ | Time Stop allows you to set up a perfect Dempsey Roll counter. |
| **Whitesnake** | ★★★★☆ | Disc steal becomes much easier to land after a White Fang stun. |
| **Anubis** | ★★★☆☆ | While good, Anubis usually pairs better with Kendo for sword synergy. |

Boxing provides the "glue" for these builds. For example, a **Star Platinum** user can use the Boxing M1s to pressure a block, use White Fang to break the guard or stun, and then follow up with a devastating heavy punch. This combination makes you a nightmare in close-quarters PvP.

<Callout type="info" title="Build Strategy">
Focus your stat points on **Strength** and **Agility** when running a boxing build. Since you will be in the enemy's face constantly, having the speed to dodge and the power to make every punch count is vital.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Boxing vs. Other Fighting Styles">

Choosing between Boxing, Karate, and Kendo depends entirely on your preferred combat role. While Boxing is the king of 1v1 stuns, other styles offer better area-of-effect (AoE) or range.

<Checklist
  id="boxing-comparison"
  title="Why Choose Boxing Over Others?"
  items={[
    "Fastest stun recovery in the game",
    "Most reliable counter move (Dempsey Roll)",
    "Simplest execution for beginners",
    "Best synergy with close-range power Stands",
    "Lower skill floor compared to Kendo"
  ]}
/>

| Style | Best For | Main Strength | Skill Ceiling |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Boxing** | 1v1 PvP | Stuns & Counters | Medium |
| **Karate** | PvE / Groups | AoE & Mobility | Medium |
| **Kendo** | Technical Play | Guard Breaking | High |

Boxing is often considered the "Meta" choice for competitive players because it removes the room for error. If you land a Liver Crusher, you are almost guaranteed to follow up with a full combo. Karate, while flashy with its dropkicks and aerial moves, can be harder to land against experienced players who know how to space properly.

<Callout type="warning" title="Range Limitation">
Always remember that Boxing has the shortest range of the three styles. If you are fighting a Kendo user, you must use your dash effectively to get inside their sword's reach, or you will be poked to death.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "Does bizarre lineage boxing change my basic attacks?",
    answer: "Yes. Equipping the boxing style changes the animations and speed of your M1 (Left Click) and M2 (Right Click) attacks, making them faster and more specialized for brawling."
  },
  {
    question: "Can I switch from Boxing to Karate later?",
    answer: "Yes, once you have learned a style, you can switch between them freely from your build menu. You do not need to pay the $100 fee again if you have already completed the quest."
  },
  {
    question: "Is Boxing good for farming NPCs?",
    answer: "While Boxing is excellent for 1v1 boss fights, Karate is generally better for farming groups of NPCs due to its superior AoE (Area of Effect) capabilities."
  },
  {
    question: "Do I lose my fighting style when I prestige?",
    answer: "Prestiging in Bizarre Lineage typically resets your fighting style. It is recommended to check the specific prestige requirements for the current 2026 update to see what items and skills are retained."
  }
]} />

<Callout type="tip" title="Final Advice">
If you are unsure which style to pick first, start with Boxing. Its reliability in PvP will help you win the duels necessary to earn more cash and experience, making it easier to unlock the other styles later.
</Callout>

</Section>
